data.description:
## Summary

The `Reconciler Heartbeat Canary` workflow (`.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge-canary.yml`)
fails on every run whenever `bridge-fsck` reports binding drift, because its
`Open binding-drift alert` step calls `rebar create` (a write) but the store was
never write-initialized in CI — so the write seam rejects it.

## Reproduction Steps

1. In a fresh checkout, mount the tickets branch with a bare
   `git worktree add -B tickets .tickets-tracker origin/tickets` (as the canary does)
   — this does NOT create `.tickets-tracker/.env-id`.
2. Run any rebar write, e.g. `rebar create bug "x"`.

- **Expected:** the write succeeds and a ticket is created.
- **Actual:** `Error: ticket system not initialized. Run 'ticket init' first.` (exit 1),
  raised by the write seam at `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py:180`, which refuses to
  append any event unless `.tickets-tracker/.env-id` exists. `.env-id` is git-ignored,
  never committed to the `tickets` branch, and is created only by `rebar init`.

## Root Cause

The canary's `Mount tickets branch as a worktree` step does a bare `git worktree add`
and never runs `rebar init`, so `.env-id` is absent. It was latent until the
binding-drift alerting step (commit `dc01c11fe`) added the first `rebar create` the
canary actually exercises; it fires whenever `bridge-fsck` sees drift. The documented
headless-CI provisioning path (`docs/architecture.md`) is `rebar init` — idempotent,
non-interactive, attaches to the existing `origin/tickets`, and seeds `.env-id`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A `rebar init` step is added to `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml` after the store is
      mounted and rebar is installed, so `.env-id` is seeded before any rebar write.
- [ ] The canary's `rebar create` / `rebar comment` / `rebar transition` write steps no
      longer fail with "ticket system not initialized".
- [ ] The step is idempotent and non-interactive (safe on every scheduled run).
- [ ] Change is pushed to Gerrit `refs/for/main` with a `rebar-ticket:` trailer.
data.title: canary: rebar create fails 'ticket system not initialized' — mount step never seeds .env-id
